1_executive_summary.key_finding: reset_admin.py is CLI-only (python -m app.gateway.auth.reset_admin), not HTTP-exposed. All privileged operations are local to the process. credential_file.write_initial_credentials uses atomic O_CREAT|O_TRUNC with mode 0600 to prevent credential exposure.
1_executive_summary.warning: SURFACE-010 must be unblocked before reset_admin.py can run (depends on init_engine_from_config)

credential_file_py.note: O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC with mode 0600 — password never world-readable

operations.name: Password hash write
operations.threat: Plaintext password in memory before hashing
operations.mitigation: Immediately hashed with dfv2 (SHA-256 + bcrypt), plaintext not stored or logged

operations.name: Token version increment
operations.threat: All existing JWTs for this user become invalid immediately
operations.mitigation: Intentional — forces all sessions to re-authenticate with new password

operations.name: needs_setup flag set
operations.threat: User must complete setup flow on next login
operations.mitigation: Expected behavior — operator informed via log message

operations.name: Credential file write
operations.mitigation: Atomic O_CREAT|O_TRUNC with mode 0600 — only process user can read

operations.name: Database user lookup
operations.mitigation: Only usable by operator with CLI access; email not returned on error

operations.name: Persistence engine init
operations.mitigation: Expected for admin reset; database must exist for reset to work

attack_vectors.scenario: Attacker with shell access to the machine runs reset_admin.py

existing_mitigations: Password written to file only, not printed
File should be deleted after operator logs in
attack_vectors.residual_risk: Operator may forget to delete file

attack_vectors.scenario: Plaintext password accessible in process memory
attack_vectors.residual_risk: Short window between input and hash

attack_vectors.scenario: Attacker attempts to use old JWT after reset
existing_mitigations: token_version increment invalidates all existing tokens
attack_vectors.residual_risk: None — token_version check enforced in get_current_user_from_request

session_factory.note: reset_admin.py will exit with error if sf is None

steps.purpose: Initialize SQLAlchemy engine and create tables if needed

steps.error_if_none: Exits with error: 'persistence engine not available'

variants: If no --email: SELECT first admin (UserRow.system_role == 'admin' LIMIT 1)
steps.error_if_not_found: Exits with error: 'user EMAIL not found' or 'no admin user found'

steps.purpose: Invalidate all existing JWTs for this user
Force user through setup flow on next login
Persist changes to database (password_hash, token_version, needs_setup)
Atomically write credential file with mode 0600

exit_codes.1: Error (user not found, engine unavailable, etc.)

8_token_version_invalidation_contract.effect: All existing JWTs become invalid immediately
8_token_version_invalidation_contract.verification_code: if user.token_version != payload.ver: raise HTTPException(401, 'Token revoked')
8_token_version_invalidation_contract.rollback_note: Password cannot be 'un-reset' — token_version is monotonically increasing

needs_setup_flag.set_by: _ensure_admin_user() on first boot (not — logs only, does not set needs_setup)
needs_setup_flag.effect: User must complete setup flow on next login

system_role_field.note: reset_admin.py does NOT modify system_role — only resets password

interaction.reset_admin: Sets needs_setup=True, does NOT change system_role
interaction.reset_then_setup: After reset, user must login with temp password, then complete setup

first_admin_creation.note: HTTP endpoint — runs when gateway is active

existing_admin_reset.note: CLI tool — does not require gateway

no_auto_admin_creation.action: Logs 'First boot detected — visit /setup', does NOT auto-create admin
no_auto_admin_creation.reason: No auto-admin — operator must explicitly create via /setup

10_first_admin_existing_admin_decision_b.decision_boundary: reset_admin.py does NOT create first admin (only resets existing). First admin creation is always via /setup HTTP endpoint.

no_http_equivalence.note: There is NO HTTP endpoint for admin password reset
no_http_equivalence.reason: Admin password reset is a privileged operation requiring host access
no_http_equivalence.alternative: Operator SSH into host and run CLI tool

no_route_registration_guarantee.grep_verification: reset_admin.py does NOT call app.add_route or app.include_router
no_route_registration_guarantee.app_py_verification: app.py does NOT import or register reset_admin routes

evidence: reset_admin.py has no FastAPI router objects
reset_admin.py is not importable from app.gateway.routers
12_no_route_registration_guarantee.conclusion: GUARANTEED: reset_admin.py registers no FastAPI routes

13_no_gateway_main_path_modification_gua.lifespan_inspection: lifespan function does NOT call reset_admin
13_no_gateway_main_path_modification_gua.conclusion: GUARANTEED: reset_admin.py does not modify gateway main path

dt001_not_required.note: reset_admin.py does NOT use user_context.py (ContextVar for auth middleware)
dt001_not_required.reason: CLI tool runs standalone, not through FastAPI middleware chain

memory_backend_handling.sf_none: reset_admin.py exits with error message if get_session_factory() returns None
memory_backend_handling.reason: Cannot reset admin without database — no user to reset

parallel_with_auth_bundle_c.can_run_parallel: YES — reset_admin needs engine, Auth Bundle C needs user_context

required_audit_trail.db_change: password_hash, token_version, needs_setup updated for target user
required_audit_trail.non_repudiation: File mode 0600 — only operator's user can read

no_audit_in_reset_admin.note: reset_admin.py does NOT write to an audit log
no_audit_in_reset_admin.stdout_message: Only prints: 'Password reset for: EMAIL' and 'Credentials written to: PATH'
no_audit_in_reset_admin.improvement_opportunity: Could add syslog/journald integration for audit trail

db_audit.token_version_increment: DB write is audit-equivalent — all old tokens invalidated
db_audit.timestamp: created_at unchanged — reset event not timestamped in DB

cases: reset with --email flag finds correct user
reset with non-existent email exits with error
reset when no admin exists exits with error
needs_setup set to True after reset
credential file written with correct content
exit code 1 on user not found
file contains correct email and password
atomic write — partial content not visible

current_blockers.SURFACE-010: BLOCKED — engine.py DT-002 must be unblocked first

recommended_sequence.2: Verify UserRow model exists at deerflow.persistence.user.model
recommended_sequence.6: Implement tests — do not proceed to file creation until tests pass
recommended_sequence.7: Implement reset_admin.py if all blockers cleared

17_implementation_order_if_authorized.when_not_authorized: This is an implementation PLAN only — no file creation until explicit authorization

18_explicit_stop_conditions.action: Do not run reset_admin.py — will exit with error
reset_admin.py catches and exits with error code 1
reset_admin.py exits with: 'persistence engine not available'

18_explicit_stop_conditions.condition: User not found (by email or first admin)
18_explicit_stop_conditions.action: reset_admin.py exits with error message and exit code 1

18_explicit_stop_conditions.condition: reset_admin.py used to modify user other than intended target
18_explicit_stop_conditions.action: Impossible — no parameter for user_id, only email or first-admin heuristic

credential_file_permissions.risk: File created with mode 0600 — verify OS honors this on Windows
credential_file_permissions.note: Windows ACLs work differently; atomic mode 0600 may not translate

no_audit_log.risk: reset_admin.py does not write to audit log
no_audit_log.impact: No forensic trail of who ran reset_admin and when

token_version_monotonic.risk: token_version can only increment, never rollback
token_version_monotonic.impact: If reset is performed accidentally, all tokens remain invalidated

memory_backend_exit.impact: Operator may think reset succeeded when it didn't
memory_backend_exit.mitigation: Error message clearly states 'persistence engine not available'
